name: Docker & Deployment
description: Docker containerization best practices, multi-stage builds, docker-compose, and deployment workflows
author: StartUpp AI IDE
rules: Always use multi-stage builds to minimize final image size -- separate build and runtime stages
Pin base image versions explicitly (e.g., node:20.11-alpine) instead of using :latest
Run containers as a non-root user; add a USER directive after installing dependencies
Use .dockerignore to exclude node_modules, .git, .env, and build artifacts from the build context
Order Dockerfile instructions from least to most frequently changing to maximize layer caching
Copy package.json and lockfile first, run npm ci, then copy source code for optimal caching
Use HEALTHCHECK directives for production containers to enable orchestrator health monitoring
Set NODE_ENV=production in the runtime stage and only install production dependencies
Never store secrets in Docker images or environment variables baked into layers; use runtime secrets
Use docker-compose profiles to separate dev, test, and production service configurations

promptTemplates.name: Generate Dockerfile
promptTemplates.description: Create a production Dockerfile for the project
promptTemplates.template: Create a production-ready multi-stage Dockerfile for a {{framework}} application. Use {{baseImage}} as the base. Include: optimized layer caching, non-root user, health check, and proper signal handling. The app listens on port {{port}}.

promptTemplates.name: Docker Compose Setup
promptTemplates.description: Generate a docker-compose.yml for local development
promptTemplates.template: Create a docker-compose.yml for local development with the following services: {{services}}. Include volume mounts for live reload, a shared network, environment variables from .env, and health checks for each service.

conventions:
Docker conventions:
- Dockerfile lives at the project root
- docker-compose.yml at the project root; docker-compose.override.yml for local dev overrides
- Use named volumes for persistent data (databases, uploads)
- Environment variables go in .env (never committed) with .env.example as a template
- Tag images with git SHA or semver for production, :latest for development only
- Document all exposed ports and required environment variables in the README
